creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
The top tourist attractions in Copenhagen are more or less all located in the city centre, but the actual best things to do in Denmark’s capital are spread out all over a much larger area 🤓  I’ve recently posted a video over on Youtube where I share 20 of the best things to do in Copenhagen - there are some famous attractions, but also quite a few more unknown spots out in the boroughs, there’s a link to the channel in my profile ✌️  Here are a few Copenhagen recommendations:

Bars & restaurants in Nørrebro: 🍻Kronborggade 3
🍻Scala
🍻Leanowski 🍻Skovbar 🍻Stefanshus 🍻Tjili Pop 🍻Lygtens Kro 🍽️Nihao YAO 🍽️Selva
🍽️Satang Thai  🍽️Leif’s Pizzeria (takeaway) 🍽️Lee’s Kitchen
 Good bars & restaurants in Vesterbro: 🍻Trows
🍻McKluud 🍻Riesen 🍻St Pauli 54 🍻Märkbar 🍻Kennedy’s 🍽️Aamanns Genbo 🍽️Gaza Grill
🍽️Folkehuset Absalon (check schedule) 🍽️Pho Hanoi 🍽️District Tonkin

Have you been to Copenhagen? Share your favourite places outside of the city centre in a comment & help other travellers who are coming to CPH 🥰

#copenhagen #copenhagenfood #denmarktravel

transcription.text: This is the worst mistake tourists make when they visit Copenhagen. I live here in Denmark's capital and I truly think it's one of the best cities in the world, especially in the summer time when the sun is shining. And as a travel blogger slash guide it hurts when I see tourists who visit Copenhagen and spend all their time in the city center. I'm not saying you shouldn't visit Strøde, Nyhavn and go on a boat tour, but what truly makes Copenhagen great is the neighborhoods outside of the city center. So make sure you go to Vesterbro and Nørrebro and check out the restaurants, cafes and bars that we locals also go to.
